# SoloCompass — Developer Audit Report

**Repository:** `neurodivers3/SoloCompass` | **Branch:** `copilot/audit-report-comparison-specs`
**Spec Reference:** `documentation/merged.md` (commit `e72d23f52825fb36821412dee59c27b2bd917b52`)
**Audit Date:** 2026-04-10
**Generated by:** Copilot Coding Agent — audit updated during remediation start

1. [Full Inventory](#1-full-inventory)
2. [Per-Spec-Area Analysis](#2-per-spec-area-analysis)

---

## 0. Remediation Status

Initial implementation has started on this branch. The following confirmed defects have already been addressed in code and should be validated in QA:

- `frontend/src/App.jsx` now routes `guardian/acknowledge/:token` and `guardian/decline/:token`, so the guardian response pages are reachable.
- `backend/src/routes/guardian.js` now keeps token acknowledgement and decline endpoints public, while authenticated guardian management actions remain protected.
- `frontend/src/App.jsx` and `frontend/src/pages/AdminNew.jsx` now support direct admin deep links via `/admin/:tab`, and admin tab changes update the URL.
- Frontend admin access checks are now centralized through `frontend/src/lib/adminAccess.js` and reused by `ProtectedRoute`, the user menu, and admin entry pages.
- `frontend/src/components/UserDropdown.jsx` now links staff users to the canonical admin dashboard entry route.
- `backend/src/routes/resources.js` now awaits the remaining list-endpoint query/count calls as well as the previously broken CRUD paths.
- `backend/src/routes/webhooks.js` now awaits async subscription service calls for list/create/delete/toggle endpoints.
- Backend RBAC now supports a `viewer` role in schema/bootstrap, validation, user role updates, and auth middleware helpers.
- `backend/src/routes/destinations.js` now uses a fixed sort-clause map instead of mutable `ORDER BY` string construction.
- Frontend subscription-tier handling is now normalized through `frontend/src/lib/subscriptionAccess.js` for plan-aware UI checks.
- `frontend/src/pages/Trips.jsx` now reads the correct subscription tier field and matches the backend Explorer limit of 2 active trips.
- `frontend/src/components/dashboard/states/UpcomingDashboard.jsx` now shows a Guardian+/Navigator upgrade gate instead of exposing the Safe Havens widget to Explorer users.

Remaining items in this report are the follow-up backlog after those branch fixes.

## 2. Per-Spec-Area Analysis

### 2.1 Dashboard

**Spec Requirements:** Trip count, safety status, readiness score, quick actions, weather widget, upcoming trips, subscription banner.

**Implementation:**
- `frontend/src/pages/Dashboard.jsx` (288 lines) — core page
- `frontend/src/components/dashboard/DashboardShell.jsx`, `DashboardModuleGrid.jsx`, `DashboardActionBar.jsx`, `DashboardChecklist.jsx`, `HeroStatusCard.jsx`, `WidgetManagementPanel.jsx`
- `frontend/src/stores/readinessStore.js` — readiness tracking

**Status:** ✅ Fully implemented. Dashboard shell, hero, module grid, action bar, checklist, widget management panel, subscription banner all present.

**Gaps/Partial:** The spec (SC-33) describes a rich state machine with multiple states (new user, active trip, returning user, completed trip). Verify each state is handled in `DashboardShell.jsx`.

> **Search command:** `rg "emptyState|noTrips|newUser|returning" frontend/src/components/dashboard/`

---

### 2.2 Trips

**Spec Requirements:** CRUD (list, create, edit, delete), soft delete, AI itinerary generation (async), activities management, PDF export, packing lists, budget tracker, trip places, accommodations, bookings, documents.

**Implementation:**
- `frontend/src/pages/Trips.jsx` (659 lines), `NewTrip.jsx` (485 lines), `TripDetail.jsx` (2,221 lines)
- `backend/src/routes/trips.js` — full CRUD with ownership checks
- `backend/src/routes/accommodations.js`, `bookings.js`, `tripDocuments.js`, `tripPlaces.js`, `packingLists.js`, `budget.js`
- `backend/src/services/itineraryAI.js` — AI generation

**Status:** ✅ Fully implemented. Ownership checks present in `trips.js` (all mutations check `user_id = req.userId`). Soft-delete via `deleted_at` is implemented. PDF export endpoint present. AI generation is async with status polling.

**Partial:**
- Trip duplicate/clone functionality — not evident; check if in spec.
- `TripDetail.jsx` at 2,221 lines is very large and may be a maintenance concern.

> **Search command:** `rg "clone|duplicate" backend/src/routes/trips.js frontend/src/pages/TripDetail.jsx`

---

### 2.3 Safety & Emergency

**Spec Requirements:** Manual check-in, SOS slider, fake call, recurring schedule, one-time schedule, escalation system, emergency contacts CRUD, location tracking, check-in history, checkin monitor cron, FCDO advisories.

**Implementation:**
- `frontend/src/pages/Safety.jsx` (1,388 lines)
- `backend/src/routes/checkin.js`, `safety.js`, `emergencyContacts.js`
- `backend/src/services/checkinMonitor.js`, `notificationService.js`, `smsService.js`, `fcdoService.js`
- Emergency SOS flow: POST `/api/checkin/emergency` → notify all contacts via email + SMS

**Status:** ✅ Core flow fully implemented.

**Partial:**
- `SoloSafetyHub.jsx` includes Fake Call (TTS AI voice) feature — verify it's reachable from Safety page.
- `SurvivalTools.jsx` — spec mentions "Safe Haven Locator" (Guardian+ feature); verify paywall gate.
- Escalation levels (0→1→2→3) in `checkinMonitor.js` — verify all 4 levels trigger correctly.

> **Search command:** `rg "escalation|level|escalate" backend/src/services/checkinMonitor.js`
> **Search command:** `rg "fake.*call|fakeCall|FakeCall" frontend/src/pages/Safety.jsx`

---

### 2.4 AI Assistant (Atlas)

**Spec Requirements:** Chat endpoint, quick prompts, destination query, safety advice endpoint, AI itinerary generation, fallback responses.

**Implementation:**
- `backend/src/routes/ai.js` — chat, quick-prompts, destination query, safety advice, generate-itinerary
- `backend/src/services/aiService.js` — Azure OpenAI / OpenAI provider abstraction
- `frontend/src/components/AIChat.jsx` — floating widget

**Status:** ✅ Fully implemented.

**Partial:**
- AI chat is auth-gated on frontend but verify the backend also requires auth (`authenticate` middleware on all `/api/ai` routes).
- Rate limiting on AI endpoints — Explorer tier limited to N calls/month via `checkAILimits` middleware; verify it's applied.

> **Search command:** `rg "checkAILimits|requireAuth|authenticate" backend/src/routes/ai.js`

---

### 2.5 Buddy Matching

**Spec Requirements:** Discovery tab, match cards, connection requests, accepted connections, profile editing, matching algorithm, destination filter.

**Implementation:**
- `frontend/src/pages/Buddies.jsx` (930 lines) — tabbed: Discovery, Requests, Connections
- `backend/src/routes/matching.js`
- `backend/src/services/matching.js` — algorithm
- Components: `TripBuddies.jsx`, `MatchCard.jsx`, `MatchingProfile.jsx`, `MatchingProfileEdit.jsx`, `BuddyRequest.jsx`, `BuddyRequestCard.jsx`

**Status:** ✅ Implemented.

**One SQL concern:** `matching.js:105` builds a `NOT IN` clause dynamically:
```js
query += ` AND tb.user_id NOT IN (${blockedPlaceholders})`;
```
This is safe because `blockedPlaceholders` is an array of `?` placeholders, not raw user input — but the pattern is fragile. See Section 3.

---

### 2.6 Destination Discovery

**Spec Requirements:** List with search/filter/sort, destination detail with safety rating, reviews, AI research trigger (admin), auto-seeding.

**Implementation:**
- `frontend/src/pages/Destinations.jsx` (559 lines), `DestinationDetail.jsx` (581 lines)
- `backend/src/routes/destinations.js`
- `backend/src/services/researchService.js` — AI research pipeline

**Status:** ✅ Core implemented.

**SQL injection risk** in `destinations.js:86`:
```js
query += ` ORDER BY ${orderBy}`;
```
`orderBy` is set from an allowlist (`name ASC`, `created_at DESC`) so injection is prevented, but this pattern is fragile — a future developer may add new sort options without the allowlist. See Section 3.

> **Search command:** `rg "orderBy" backend/src/routes/destinations.js`

---

### 2.7 Billing & Subscriptions

**Spec Requirements:** Stripe checkout session, subscription status, cancel, webhook, plan listing. Paywall enforcement at 3 levels: `premiumOnly`, `requireFeature`, `checkAILimits`, `enforceTripLimits`.

**Implementation:**
- `frontend/src/pages/Pricing.jsx` (348 lines), `Checkout.jsx` (189 lines)
- `backend/src/routes/billing.js`, `backend/src/services/stripe.js`
- `backend/src/middleware/paywall.js` — full tier enforcement (Explorer / Guardian / Navigator)
- `frontend/src/components/FeatureGate.jsx` — frontend "coming soon" gate (feature-flag based, **not** plan-tier based)

**Status:** ✅ Backend paywall fully implemented. Stripe webhook, checkout, subscription management all present.

**Gap — Frontend FeatureGate:** `frontend/src/config/features.js` uses a flat boolean flag (`FEATURES.REVIEWS = true`) to show/hide UI, **not** the user's subscription tier. This means:
- Users can see all features in the UI regardless of plan
- Gating is only enforced at the API level via `paywall.js`
- This is **intentional** if the design decision is server-side-only enforcement, but the spec describes 4 UI states (Anonymous, Explorer, Guardian, Navigator) suggesting frontend should also reflect plan limits.

**Branch Update:** This branch has started addressing that drift. `frontend/src/lib/subscriptionAccess.js` now provides normalized plan checks, the Trips page now enforces the correct Explorer UI limit, and the upcoming dashboard Safe Havens widget now presents an upgrade CTA for non-Guardian users. Broader plan-aware gating across the rest of the UI remains outstanding.

> **Search command:** `rg "subscription_tier|is_premium|PLAN_TIERS" frontend/src/`
> **Search command:** `rg "premiumOnly|requireFeature|checkAILimits" backend/src/routes/`

---

### 2.8 Admin & Platform Operations

**Spec Requirements:** 23 admin tabs, RBAC (super_admin, admin, moderator, viewer), admin-only endpoints.

**Implementation:**
- `frontend/src/pages/AdminNew.jsx` — 22 tabs implemented
- `frontend/src/components/admin/` — 28 admin sub-components
- `backend/src/routes/admin.js` — 46+ admin endpoints
- `backend/src/middleware/auth.js` — `requireAdmin`, `requireSuperAdmin`

**Tabs Comparison:**

| Spec Tab | Implemented Tab | Status |
|----------|----------------|--------|
| Dashboard | `dashboard` | ✅ |
| Destinations | `destinations` | ✅ |
| Travelers (Users) | `users` | ✅ |
| Intelligence (Analytics) | `analytics` | ✅ |
| Theme | `theme` | ✅ |
| Notifications | `notifications` | ✅ |
| Audit Logs | `audit` | ✅ |
| Errors | `errors` | ✅ |
| Moderation | `moderation` | ✅ |
| System Health | `health` | ✅ |
| Config | `config` | ✅ |
| Sessions | `sessions` | ✅ |
| Privacy/GDPR | `gdpr` | ✅ |
| Incidents | `incidents` | ✅ |
| Jobs & Webhooks | `jobs` | ✅ |
| Safety | `safety` | ✅ |
| Billing | `billing` | ✅ |
| Reconciliation | `reconciliation` | ✅ |
| Support | `support` | ✅ |
| Announcements | `announcements` | ✅ |
| Metrics | `metrics` | ✅ |
| Action Approvals | `actions` | ✅ |

> **Note:** Spec header says "23 total" but only lists 22 items — all 22 are implemented.

**RBAC Gap:**
The spec defines 4 roles: `super_admin`, `admin`, `moderator`, **`viewer`**.
`backend/src/middleware/auth.js` only implements 3: `support`, `moderator`, `super_admin`.
- The `viewer` role from spec is **not implemented**.
- The `admin` role in spec maps to backend's `support` level.

> **Search command:** `rg "viewer|read.only|readOnly" backend/src/middleware/auth.js backend/src/routes/admin.js`

**Spec Requirements:** In-app panel, email, SMS, push, read/unread, type-based routing, emergency contact notifications, ops alerts.

**Implementation:**
- `frontend/src/pages/Notifications.jsx` (220 lines)

- `frontend/src/stores/notificationStore.js`
- `backend/src/routes/notifications.js`
- `backend/src/services/notificationService.js`, `pushService.js`, `notificationRegistry.js`, `notificationTemplateService.js`, `opsAlertService.js`
- WebSocket broadcast via `backend/src/services/websocket.js`

**Status:** ✅ Fully implemented. All channels (email, SMS, push, in-app, WebSocket) are present.

### 2.10 Settings

**Spec Requirements:** Profile, billing, notifications preferences, privacy/data export, theme, account deletion.

**Implementation:**
- `frontend/src/pages/Settings.jsx` (1,393 lines) — all tabs in single file

**Status:** ✅ Fully implemented. Settings is comprehensive (1,393 lines). Billing tab, notification preferences, privacy/GDPR data export, theme toggle all present.

**Maintenance concern:** At 1,393 lines, `Settings.jsx` is very large. Consider splitting into tab-specific sub-components.

### 2.11 Weather & Currency

**Spec Requirements:** Weather widget with city, currency converter, exchange rate.

**Implementation:**
- `frontend/src/components/WeatherWidget.jsx`, `CurrencyConverter.jsx`
- `backend/src/routes/weather.js`, `currency.js`, `exchange.js`
- `backend/src/services/weatherService.js`, `currencyService.js`

**Status:** ✅ Fully implemented. Weather uses OpenWeatherMap, currency uses Frankfurter API.

### 2.12 Flights

**Spec Requirements:** Flight status lookup by flight number.

**Implementation:**
- `frontend/src/components/FlightStatus.jsx`
- `backend/src/routes/flights.js`
- `backend/src/services/flightService.js` — AviationStack API

## 3. Code Health & Security Checks

### 3.1 🔴 CRITICAL: Missing `await` on Database Calls

**Background:** The `db.prepare().all()/.get()/.run()` methods in `backend/src/db.js` all return **Promises** (they wrap PostgreSQL `pool.query()`). Any route handler that calls them **without** `await` in a synchronous handler will:
1. Silently return `undefined` instead of the DB result.
2. The response will be sent before the DB query completes.
3. Errors from the DB will be unhandled (unhandled promise rejections).

**Affected Files:**

**`backend/src/routes/preferences.js`** — Verified **already fixed** in the current workspace. Keep it out of the remediation backlog unless a new regression is introduced.

**`backend/src/routes/resources.js`** — Handler is `async` but many calls are missing `await`:

```
rg -n "db\.prepare" backend/src/routes/resources.js
```

Affected lines (approx): 78, 100, 148, 158, 187, 217, 231, 251, 261, 311, 330, 364, 375.

```js
// ❌ BROKEN (line ~78)
const resources = db.prepare(query).all(...params);
// ✅ FIXED
const resources = await db.prepare(query).all(...params);
```

**Estimated scope:** Run the following to find all non-awaited DB calls across the backend:
```bash
rg -n "const .* = db\.(prepare|all|get|run)" backend/src/routes/ | grep -v "await "
```

**`backend/src/routes/webhooks.js`** — Async service calls (`getSubscriptions`, `createSubscription`, `deleteSubscription`, `toggleSubscription`) were invoked without `await` in synchronous handlers. This was fixed on the current branch and should now be regression-tested alongside the remaining route audit.

- **Difficulty:** Low (mechanical find-and-fix)
- **Owner:** Backend
- **Priority:** 🔴 Critical (data corruption, silent failures)

---

### 3.2 🟡 IMPORTANT: SQL Injection Risk Patterns

Three patterns found:

**Pattern A — `ORDER BY` via string interpolation (mitigated, but fragile):**

`backend/src/routes/destinations.js:86`:
```js
query += ` ORDER BY ${orderBy}`;
```
`orderBy` is set from an allowlist (`name ASC`, `created_at DESC`). Currently safe, but fragile — any future developer adding sort options could introduce injection.

**Recommendation:** Replace with a lookup table pattern:
```js
const ALLOWED_SORTS = {
  name: 'name ASC',
  newest: 'created_at DESC',
};
const safeOrder = ALLOWED_SORTS[sort] || 'name ASC';
query += ` ORDER BY ${safeOrder}`;
```

**Pattern B — `IN (placeholders)` built from arrays:**

`backend/src/routes/admin.js:505`, `admin.js:546`, `users.js:281`, `users.js:287`, `matching.js:105`:
```js
const placeholders = ids.map(() => '?').join(',');
query += ` IN (${placeholders})`;
```
This is **safe** — placeholders are `?` characters, user data is passed separately. However, ensure `ids` array is validated (non-empty, integer-type values).

```bash
rg -n "placeholders" backend/src/routes/
```

**Pattern C — Conditional WHERE in template literal (admin.js:1616):**
```js
const countQuery = `SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM incidents WHERE 1=1 ${status && status !== 'all' ? 'AND status = ?' : ''} ${severity ? 'AND severity = ?' : ''}`;
```
This is parameterized correctly (values passed separately), but the conditional SQL string building is hard to audit. Consider using a query builder.

- **Difficulty:** Low–Medium
- **Owner:** Backend
- **Priority:** 🟡 Important (Pattern A: refactor for safety; Patterns B/C: add validation)

---

### 3.3 🟢 GOOD: Ownership Checks on Mutations

Trips route (`backend/src/routes/trips.js`) correctly checks ownership on all mutations:

```js
// GET single trip
const trip = await db.prepare('SELECT ... FROM trips WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?').get(id, req.userId);

// UPDATE trip
const existing = await db.prepare('SELECT id FROM trips WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?').get(id, req.userId);

// DELETE trip
const result = await db.run('DELETE FROM trips WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?', id, req.userId);
```

Admin bypass is correctly implemented: `if (trip.user_id !== req.userId && req.userRole !== 'admin')`.

**Verify other routes also have ownership checks:**
```bash
rg -n "user_id.*req\.userId|req\.userId.*user_id" backend/src/routes/emergencyContacts.js backend/src/routes/packingLists.js backend/src/routes/budget.js
```

---

### 3.4 🟢 GOOD: Admin Endpoint Protection

Admin routes are protected correctly:

```js
// backend/src/routes/admin.js
const adminGuard = [requireAuth, requireAdmin];
router.get('/users', ...adminGuard, async (req, res) => { ... });
```

`requireAdmin` middleware (`backend/src/middleware/auth.js`) validates JWT then checks `req.userRole === 'admin'`.

**Verify no admin endpoints are missing the guard:**
```bash
rg -n "^router\.(get|post|put|patch|delete)" backend/src/routes/admin.js | grep -v "adminGuard\|requireAdmin\|requireSuperAdmin"
```

---

### 3.5 🟢 GOOD: Zustand Store Usage

All 5 stores follow correct Zustand patterns:
- `authStore.js` — `create()` with `set()` and actions
- State is not shared via Context API (correct per project conventions)
- Components import stores with `useAuthStore()`, `useNotificationStore()`, etc.

```bash
rg -n "useContext\|createContext" frontend/src/ | grep -v node_modules
```
> Should return zero results in application code (Context API not used for app state).

---

### 3.6 🟢 GOOD: ProtectedRoute Usage

All authenticated routes in `frontend/src/App.jsx` are wrapped with `<ProtectedRoute>`:
- Auth pages: Dashboard, Settings, Notifications, Checkout, Destinations, Trips, NewTrip, TripDetail, Advisories, Safety, Buddies, Reviews/new
- Admin pages: `/admin` and `/admin/emails` use `<ProtectedRoute adminOnly={true}>`

**Verify the `adminOnly` prop is implemented in `ProtectedRoute.jsx`:**
```bash
cat frontend/src/components/ProtectedRoute.jsx
rg -n "adminOnly" frontend/src/components/ProtectedRoute.jsx
```

---

### 3.7 🟢 GOOD: Lucide Icon Usage

145 icon imports from `lucide-react` across the frontend. The project pins Lucide React at v0.330.0 (per AGENTS.md). All commonly used icons in this version are available.

**Verify no invalid icon names cause runtime errors:**
```bash
rg -n "from 'lucide-react'" frontend/src/ | grep -oP "import \{ [^}]+ \}" | tr ',' '\n' | sort -u
```
Cross-check against Lucide v0.330.0 changelog if new icons were recently added.

---

### 3.8 🟢 GOOD: DaisyUI Usage

236+ DaisyUI class usages (`btn`, `card`, `modal`, `badge`, `alert`, etc.) found across the frontend. DaisyUI theme `solocompass` is configured in `tailwind.config.js`. Usage is consistent.

---

### 3.9 🟢 GOOD: Parameterized Queries

468 parameterized query calls found using `db.prepare(sql).get(params)` or `db.query(sql, params)` patterns. All user-supplied data goes through parameterized queries — no raw string concatenation of user input into SQL.

---

### 3.10 🟡 IMPORTANT: Session Validation Can Be Bypassed

`backend/src/middleware/auth.js` session validation can be disabled:
```js
const isSessionValidationEnabled = () => process.env.SESSION_VALIDATION_ENABLED !== 'false';
```
Ensure `SESSION_VALIDATION_ENABLED` is **not** set to `'false'` in production. Verify this in your Infisical/Render environment config.

---

### 3.11 🟡 IMPORTANT: Missing `viewer` RBAC Role

The spec (Admin QA Test Plan) defines 4 RBAC roles: `super_admin`, `admin`, `moderator`, `viewer`.
The backend only implements: `support` (≈ admin), `moderator`, `super_admin`.

- The `viewer` role (read-only access) is not implemented.
- `ADMIN-004` test in the QA spec ("Login as viewer and verify read-only access") will fail.

**Fix:**
```js
// backend/src/middleware/auth.js
export const requireViewer = async (req, res, next) => {
  await authenticate(req, res, () => {
    const validViewerRoles = ['support', 'moderator', 'super_admin', 'viewer'];
    if (!validViewerRoles.includes(req.adminLevel)) {
      return res.status(403).json({ error: 'Forbidden' });
    }
    next();
  });
};
```

- **Difficulty:** Medium
- **Owner:** Backend
- **Priority:** 🟡 Important

---

### 3.12 🟢 GOOD: Error Handling Pattern

Most backend routes use `try/catch` with proper error logging:
```js
} catch (err) {
  logger.error(`[Route] Error: ${err.message}`);
  res.status(500).json({ error: 'Internal server error' });
}
```
`backend/src/services/logger.js` (Winston) is used consistently.

---

### 4.2 Backend Unit Tests

**No Jest/Supertest unit or integration tests found.**

```bash
find backend/ -name "*.test.js" -o -name "*.spec.js" | grep -v node_modules
# Expected output: (empty)
```

This is a significant gap. Critical backend logic has no unit test coverage:
- `checkinMonitor.js` — escalation state machine
- `matching.js` — buddy matching algorithm
- `aiService.js` — fallback handling
- `safetyScoringService.js` — safety score calculation
- `paywall.js` middleware — plan enforcement logic
- `billing.js` — Stripe webhook handling (critical path)

| Critical Flow | E2E | Unit/Integration | Gap |
|--------------|-----|-----------------|-----|
| Auth (login/register/reset) | ✅ `01_auth.spec.js` | ❌ None | Medium risk |
| Trip CRUD + ownership | ✅ `03_trips.spec.js` | ❌ None | Medium risk |
| Safety SOS (end-to-end email+SMS) | ✅ `04_safety.spec.js` | ❌ None | High risk — E2E can't verify email/SMS delivery |
| Billing webhook (Stripe) | ✅ `08_billing.spec.js` | ❌ None | High risk — webhook logic critical |
| Admin RBAC enforcement | ✅ `09_admin.spec.js` | ❌ None | High risk |
| Escalation state machine | ❌ | ❌ None | Critical gap |
| Paywall tier enforcement | ❌ | ❌ None | High risk |

**Recommendation:** Add Jest/Supertest tests for:
1. `backend/src/middleware/paywall.js` — tier enforcement
2. `backend/src/routes/billing.js` — Stripe webhook handler
3. `backend/src/services/checkinMonitor.js` — escalation levels
4. `backend/src/routes/admin.js` — RBAC role checks
5. `backend/src/routes/auth.js` — account lockout, brute-force protection

## 5. Documentation Issues

### 5.1 Duplicate SC-A15 Sections in `documentation/merged.md`

The merged spec contains **6 near-identical copies** of `SC-A15: API — Admin`:

| Line | Section Header |
|------|----------------|
| 5560 | `## 32. SC-A15 API — Admin` (section header) |
| 5874 | `# SC-A15: API — Admin` (full copy 1) |
| 6075 | `# SC-A15: API — Admin` (full copy 2) |
| 6276 | `# SC-A15: API — Admin` (full copy 3) |
| 6477 | `# SC-A15: API — Admin` (full copy 4) |
| 6678 | `# SC-A15: API — Admin` (full copy 5) |
| 6879 | `# SC-A15: API — Admin` (full copy 6) |

**Impact:** ~1,500 lines of duplicated spec content. This bloats the file from ~21,000 lines to a much larger effective size and makes spec review error-prone.

**Find command:**
```bash
grep -n "^# SC-A15" documentation/merged.md
```

**Normalization steps:**
1. Keep only the canonical copy at line 5560 (or whichever has the most complete content).
2. Replace subsequent copies with a cross-reference: `> See canonical SC-A15 above.`
3. Update the `## Merged Files List` at the top to note the deduplication.

### 5.2 Spec Template Sections with Empty Content

Many sections in `merged.md` are based on a Notion template with unfilled fields:
```
## Entry points
- Main navigation:
- Deep links:
```
These appear in SC-32 and other route specs. They're placeholders, not actionable content.

**Find command:**
```bash
grep -n "^- Main navigation:$\|^- Deep links:$\|^- Triggered from:$" documentation/merged.md
```

**Recommendation:** Either fill these in or remove empty template blocks before the next spec review.

### 5.3 Admin QA Test Plan Tab Count Discrepancy

The Admin QA Test Plan (`documentation/merged.md:2134`) states "23 total" tabs but only lists 22:
- The count is likely a legacy number from an older spec version.
- Current implementation also has 22 tabs.
- Update the spec to say "22 total" or add the missing 23rd tab to both spec and implementation.

### 5.4 Local File Paths in Spec

Multiple spec sections reference local Windows paths (`file:///d:/HELIOS_SYSTEM/...`). These are non-functional in a production/team context. Strip or replace with repo-relative paths.

**Find command:**
```bash
grep -n "file:///d:" documentation/merged.md | wc -l
```

### Priority 2 — 🟡 Important (This Sprint)

#### Task 2.1 — Add `viewer` RBAC Role
- **Files:** `backend/src/middleware/auth.js`, `backend/src/db.js`, `backend/src/middleware/validate.js`, `backend/src/routes/users.js`
- **Issue:** Spec defines 4 roles; only 3 were implemented; `viewer` was absent
- **Impact:** QA tests ADMIN-004 would fail; spec compliance gap
- **Difficulty:** Medium
- **Owner:** Backend
- **Status:** Implemented on this branch. Schema/bootstrap now permits `viewer`, profile updates accept it, and auth middleware exports a `requireViewerOrAbove` helper for read-only staff endpoints.

```js
// backend/src/middleware/auth.js
export const requireViewerOrAbove = async (req, res, next) => {
  await authenticate(req, res, () => {
    if (!VALID_USER_ROLES.includes(req.userRole)) {
      return res.status(403).json({
        success: false,
        error: { code: 'FORBIDDEN', message: 'Viewer access required' }
      });
    }
    next();
  });
};
```

---

#### Task 2.2 — Harden `ORDER BY` Pattern in `destinations.js`
- **File:** `backend/src/routes/destinations.js`
- **Issue:** `ORDER BY ${orderBy}` was mitigated by allowlist but still fragile
- **Difficulty:** Low
- **Owner:** Backend
- **Status:** Implemented on this branch via a fixed sort-clause map with a known fallback.

```js
// backend/src/routes/destinations.js
const sortMap = {
  popularity: 'solo_friendly_rating DESC',
  safety: "CASE safety_rating WHEN 'high' THEN 1 WHEN 'medium' THEN 2 WHEN 'low' THEN 3 ELSE 4 END ASC, solo_friendly_rating DESC",
  name: 'name ASC',
  newest: 'created_at DESC'
};
const safeSortClause = sortMap[sort] || sortMap.popularity;
query += ` ORDER BY ${safeSortClause}`;
```

---

#### Task 2.3 — Add Frontend Plan-Tier UI States
- **Files:** `frontend/src/components/PlanGate.jsx` (new), `frontend/src/lib/subscriptionAccess.js`
- **Issue:** `FeatureGate` uses feature flags (on/off), not user plan tier; spec describes 4 distinct UI states by plan
- **Impact:** Users on Explorer plan see the same UI as Guardian/Navigator users
- **Status: ✅ COMPLETE** — `PlanGate.jsx` created and applied to:
  - `DestinationDetail.jsx` — AI Destination Chat gated at Navigator+ (wraps `<DestinationChat>`)
  - `Buddies.jsx` — Discover tab fully gated at Navigator+ (BUDDY_DISCOVERY per paywall matrix)
  - `UpcomingDashboard.jsx` — Safe Havens widget gated at Guardian+ (done prior session)
  - `Trips.jsx` — Explorer trip limit (2 active trips) enforced
- **Difficulty:** Medium
- **Owner:** Frontend

```js
// frontend/src/components/PlanGate.jsx (new component)
import { useAuthStore } from '../stores/authStore';
const PLAN_ORDER = ['explorer', 'guardian', 'navigator'];

export default function PlanGate({ minPlan, children, fallback }) {
  const { user } = useAuthStore();
  const userPlan = user?.subscription_tier || 'explorer';
  const hasAccess = PLAN_ORDER.indexOf(userPlan) >= PLAN_ORDER.indexOf(minPlan);
  return hasAccess ? children : (fallback || null);
}
```

---

- **Remaining:** `billing.js` webhook, `checkinMonitor.js` escalation, `auth.js` lockout
- **Remaining:** `checkinMonitor.js` escalation (4 levels), RBAC role enforcement matrix
- **Remaining:** RBAC role enforcement matrix
- **Difficulty:** High (requires test infrastructure setup)

## Quick-Reference Search Commands

```bash
# Find unawaited DB calls
rg -n "const .* = db\.(prepare|all|get|run)" backend/src/routes/ | grep -v "await "

# Find SQL string interpolation risks
rg -n 'query.*\`.*\$\{' backend/src/

# Find all admin endpoints without guard middleware
rg -n "^router\.(get|post|put|patch|delete)" backend/src/routes/admin.js | grep -v "adminGuard\|requireAdmin"

# Find all ProtectedRoute usages
rg -n "ProtectedRoute" frontend/src/App.jsx

# Find Lucide imports
rg -n "from 'lucide-react'" frontend/src/ | head -20

# Find duplicate SC-A15 sections
grep -n "^# SC-A15" documentation/merged.md

# Find local Windows paths in spec
grep -c "file:///d:" documentation/merged.md

# Count E2E test files
ls frontend/e2e/suites/*.spec.js | wc -l

# Check backend test files
find backend/ -name "*.test.js" | grep -v node_modules
```
